title: Ceriops tagal
intro: Ceriops tagal, also known as the mangrove red gum, is a species of mangrove tree that belongs to the family Rhizophoraceae. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, which can help in treating wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ceriops tagal include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Ceriops tagal is classified in the kingdom Plantae, and more specifically in the family Rhizophoraceae, which is a family of mangrove trees. The genus Ceriops is part of the family Rhizophoraceae and includes several species of mangroves. The species Ceriops tagal is a type of mangrove tree that is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the Indo-Pacific area. Ceriops tagal is commonly known as the river mangrove or seaforthia. The classification of Ceriops tagal is further divided into different ranks, including kingdom, family, genus, and species.
